title: Rich Velvety Japanese Pork Ramen Shio
description: A floral and velvety Japanese ramen dish featuring minced garlic and long-fermentation-dough, originating from Hokkaido, and suitable for diabetic-friendly diets.

ingredients.name: pork bones
ingredients.notes: you can use pork neck bones or rib bones

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit to roast the pork bones.
Roast the pork bones in the oven for 30 minutes to enhance the flavor.
In a large pot, heat the sesame oil over medium heat and saute the minced garlic until fragrant.
Add the roasted pork bones, soy sauce, and sake to the pot and pour in 4 quarts of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 4 hours to make the rich pork broth.
Slice the pork loin into thin strips and cook in the broth for 10 minutes, or until cooked through.
Cook the long-fermentation-dough noodles according to the package instructions and drain well.
Assemble the ramen by placing the noodles in a bowl, adding slices of pork, and ladling the hot broth over the top.
Garnish with green onions and pickled ginger to serve.
Serve immediately and enjoy the rich and velvety Japanese pork ramen shio.
